g.data-narration: Welcome to HDL Models of Combinational Circuits. Today we explore Adders, the fundamental building blocks of digital arithmetic.
An adder is a combinational circuit that performs binary addition. It takes bit inputs and produces sum and carry outputs.
g.text: A combinational circuit that performs binary addition of two numbers.
Inputs: A, B (and Cin for full adder) | Outputs: Sum (S), Carry (C)

g.data-narration: There are three main types: Half Adder adds two bits, Full Adder adds three bits, and N-Bit Adder handles multiple bits.
The Half Adder adds two single bits. The Sum output uses XOR gate, while Carry uses AND gate.
In VHDL, the Half Adder is modeled behaviorally. The Sum uses XOR operation and Carry uses AND operation.
g.text: port (A, B: in bit; S, C: out bit);

g.data-narration: In Verilog, the Half Adder uses assign statements with XOR for sum and AND/OR for carry output.
g.text: module half_adder(input A, B, output S, C_out);
assign S = A ^ B; assign C_out = (A & B) | ((A == 1'b0) & (B == 1'b0));

g.data-narration: The Full Adder adds three bits: A, B, and carry input Cin. It produces Sum and Carry output using XOR and OR gates.
The VHDL model of Full Adder uses nested XOR for sum and expression with OR gates for carry output.
g.text: port (A, B, Cin: in bit; S, Cout: out bit);
Cout <= (A and B) or (A and Cin) or (B and Cin);

g.data-narration: The Verilog Full Adder uses assign statements: triple XOR for sum output and expression with AND/OR for carry.
g.text: module full_adder(input A, B, Cin, output S, Cout);
assign S = A ^ B ^ Cin; assign Cout = (A & B) | (A & Cin) | (B & Cin);

g.data-narration: An N-Bit Adder chains multiple full adders in series. Each stage's carry output feeds into the next stage's carry input.
g.text: • Each FA output Cout → next FA input Cin
• For N-bit: chain N full adders together

g.data-narration: The VHDL N-Bit Adder uses component instantiation and generate statements to create multiple full adder stages.
g.text: FA0: FullAdder port map(A(0), B(0), CIN, Sum(0), CarryOut(0));
FA(i+1): FullAdder port map(A(i+1), B(i+1), CarryOut(i), Sum(i+1), Cout);

g.data-narration: The Verilog implementation uses generate statements to instantiate half adders and full adders in a loop.
g.text: for (j = 0; j < 3; j = j + 1) begin : FA_gen

g.data-narration: Let's review the key differences: Half Adder has 2 inputs, Full Adder has 3 inputs, and N-Bit chains multiple full adders.
Adders are used in processors, DSP chips, FPGAs, and ASIC design for arithmetic operations in CPUs and microcontrollers.
In this lesson, we learned HDL modeling of adders. Master these concepts to design complex digital systems and arithmetic circuits.
g.text: Conclusion: HDL Modeling is Essential for Digital Design
Practice VHDL and Verilog to master combinational circuit design!
